name.description: The name of the package to be installed via apt-get.

bin_name.description: The name of the binary. This is optional and can be used if the binary name differs from the package name.

entry_file.description: The path to the bash script file that serves as the entry point for the Step.

name.description: The name of the package to be installed via Homebrew.

bin_name.description: The name of the binary. This is optional and can be used if the binary name differs from the package name.

name.description: The name of the binary or tool to check for. If it is not present on the system, the Step will fail before running.

manager.description: The package manager used to handle the dependency.

items.description: A Homebrew dependency required by the Step.

brew.description: List of Homebrew dependencies required by the Step.

items.description: An apt-get dependency required by the Step.

apt_get.description: List of apt-get dependencies required by the Step.

items.description: A dependency that is only checked for existence.

check_only.description: List of dependencies that are only checked for existence.

storage_uri.description: The URI where the executable binary is stored.

hash.description: The hash of the executable binary for integrity verification.

package_name.description: The name of the Go package that serves as the entry point for the Step.

ContainerReferenceConfigModel.description: Additional configuration for a container reference.

recreate.description: If true, the current container instance is discarded and a fresh one is started for this Step.

ContainerReferenceModel.description: A reference to a container. Can be specified as a container ID string or as an object with the container ID as the key and optional configuration as the value.

oneOf.description: The ID of the container as defined in the top-level `containers` map.
The container ID with optional configuration.

title.description: The human-readable title of the Step.

summary.description: A short summary of what the Step does.

description.description: A more detailed overview of the Step's function and configuration.

website.description: A web URL where users can find more information about the Step or the tools and services it implements.

source_code_url.description: The URL of the repository of the Step's source code.

support_url.description: A URL where users can get support for the Step.

published_at.description: The date and time when the Step was published to the Step Library. This is auto-generated and should not be set manually.

source.description: The source code repository and commit information of the Step.

.*.description: A URL pointing to a Step asset such as an icon image.

asset_urls.description: URLs of assets associated with the Step, such as icon images. This is auto-generated and only set in the spec.json file in the Step Library.

host_os_tags.description: This property defines the host operating systems the Step is compatible with. For example, `linux` or `macos`.

project_type_tags.description: This property defines the project type category of the Step. For example, `flutter` or `ios`.

items.description: A functional category tag. For example, `utility`, `test`, or `notification`.

type_tags.description: This property defines the functional category of the Step. For example, `utility`, `test`, or `notification`.

dependencies.description: The dependencies required by the Step.

toolkit.description: The toolkit used by the Step.

deps.description: The dependencies required by the Step.

is_requires_admin_user.description: If this property is true, the Step requires admin user privileges to run.

is_always_run.description: If this property is true, the Step will always run, even if a previous Step in the Workflow failed.

is_skippable.description: If this property is true, the build won't fail even if this Step fails. For example, if a Step restoring a cache archive fails, you might still want to run the build.

run_if.description: This property sets conditions for running a Step. It requires boolean value or a valid Go template expression.

timeout.description: This property defines a time limit for a Step: if the Step runs longer than the defined time, the Step fails. Define the limit in seconds.

no_output_timeout.description: This property defines a time limit for a Step that produces no output: if the Step runs for the defined number of seconds without producing any output, the Step fails. Define the limit in seconds.

meta.description: Additional metadata related to the Step.

execution_container.description: The execution container for this Step. The Step runs inside the specified Docker container.

service_containers.description: Service containers to run alongside this Step as background services.

items.description: An input parameter of the Step.
An output parameter of the Step.

git.description: The Git repository URL of the Step's source code.

commit.description: The commit hash for the version tag of the Step's current version.

bash.description: The Bash toolkit configuration for the Step.

go.description: The Go toolkit configuration for the Step.
